_comment: SLICE_S2_CONTRACT.md §4 — additive manifest file, union-merge friendly, sibling to backend/domain-core/Svac.DomainCore/Config/manifests/domain-core.config.json (never edits to that file's rows). CORRECTION 1 (§13 ratification) applied verbatim: v0 default model is the best-available Claude, claude-opus-4-8, in BOTH the allowlist's models[] and the routing policy's default_chain[0].model — never a smaller/cheaper model as the router-wide default (CLAUDE.md: no silent downgrades).

entries.consumer: Svac.AimlRouter.Routing.Resolver (Automatic + Explicit paths) — OQ-A ratified conservative posture: ceiling pseudonymous, dpa_signed false, until counsel's L-1/L-3 confirms the Anthropic DPA + retention terms
Svac.AimlRouter.AimlRouterService per-hop invocation timeout; bounds [5, 300]; feeds the Timeout failure kind

Svac.DomainCore.Quota.QuotaService.Consume (10A `quota.<quotaKey>.cap` convention, QuotaService.cs:24) for quotaKey=AimlRouterQuotaKeys.CallDaily="aiml.call.daily" (SLICE_S2_CONTRACT.md §5) — the actual runtime key AimlRouterService.ConsumeDailyBudget's budget circuit breaker resolves. Renamed from the contract §4 table's literal `aiml.daily_call_ceiling` (a build-phase fix, backend/tests/Svac.Tests.AimlRouter/BudgetCapConfigKeyWiringTests.cs pins it): that spelling collided with nothing QuotaService.Consume ever reads, so every InvokeAsync call threw KeyNotFoundException. Same v0 value (10000), same scope/requiresReason — the fix is the key's spelling, not its shape.
